details:
Five IN_PROGRESS claims are reclaimable: their owning controllers have not heart-beat for well beyond the
240-minute stale threshold, so the claims are abandoned by dead controllers and are holding queue state.

reclaim-stale --dry-run (heartbeat age at capture):
- row 27 [EPIC_02][STORY_03][SUBSTORY_01] Add race and creatureClass properties -- ctrl-vm-4479, ~391 min
- row 29 [EPIC_02][STORY_03][SUBSTORY_03] Add usesArchetypeComposition helper   -- ctrl-vm-4479, ~391 min
- row 21 [EPIC_02][STORY_01][SUBSTORY_02] #669 Harden C...                       -- ctrl-vm-4026, ~841 min
- row 25 [EPIC_02][STORY_02][SUBSTORY_01] #673 Harden W...                       -- ctrl-vm-4024, ~369 min
- row 22 [EPIC_05][STORY_01][SUBSTORY_01] #670 Define a...                       -- ctrl-vm-4024, ~376 min

Rows 27 and 29 are additionally queue DRIFT: their deliverables are already merged on main and satisfy
their acceptance criteria, yet the rows remain IN_PROGRESS under the dead controller's expired claims.
Verified on main:
- row 27 acceptance ("Both CCreature and CPlayer can store race and creatureClass; legacy default null"):
  src/object/CCreature.h:68-69 declare V_PROPERTY race (shared_ptr<CCreatureRace>) and creatureClass
  (shared_ptr<CCreatureClass>) with get/set; both default to null. CCreatureRace.h and CCreatureClass.h exist.
- row 29 acceptance ("legacy creatures with no archetypes unchanged; composed creatures use composed calc"):
  src/object/CCreature.cpp:971 -- bool CCreature::usesArchetypeComposition() { return race != nullptr ||
  creatureClass != nullptr; } -- and it gates composed calculation at CCreature.cpp:577.

Impact:
- Because these EPIC_02 STORY_03 composition-runtime rows appear IN_PROGRESS (not DONE), the EPIC_04/05
  race/class authoring cluster looked dependency-gated for hours, when in fact the runtime (properties,
  usesArchetypeComposition, CCreatureRace/CCreatureClass) plus the race-aware loader (row 58) are all merged.
  A live controller repeatedly found "no safely-claimable P1 slot" and idled, waiting on work that was
  already done but never marked DONE by the controller that died mid-completion.
- The abandoned claims also block reclaim-free capacity accounting across the fleet.

Recommended reconciliation:
- Reconcile rows 27 and 29 to DONE (their work is merged and verified), then treat the EPIC_04/05 race
  authoring cluster as unblocked (serialize the first creature_races.json / creature_classes.json schema
  author, then fan out).
- Reclaim rows 21/25/22 (inspect any abandoned worker branch/PR for recoverable work first) and re-drive
  or release them.
- Consider shortening the heartbeat/lease so a dead controller's claims free up well before 6-14 hours.
fingerprint: abandoned reclaimable claims from dead controllers hold queue state runtime rows drift not marked done
impact: The EPIC_04/05 race/class authoring cluster appeared dependency-gated for hours because the merged EPIC_02 composition-runtime rows 27/29 were never marked DONE by a controller that died mid-completion; live controllers idled on already-finished work
suggestedImprovement: Reconcile rows 27/29 to DONE (verified merged); reclaim 21/25/22 after inspecting for recoverable branches; shorten heartbeat/lease so dead-controller claims free up before many hours
summary: Five IN_PROGRESS claims are abandoned by dead controllers (stale 369-841 min); rows 27/29 are drift already merged on main, making the race cluster look gated
